name: Llanos de Olivenza — circular from Valverde de Leganés
name_original: Llanos de Olivenza — circular desde Valverde de Leganés
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valverde de Leganés, located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, traverse a serene rural landscape. The area features extensive cultivated fields, olive groves, and dehesas, offering varied scenery for cyclists. Terrain includes areas with varying elevations, providing both challenging ascents and descents, alongside routes with mostly paved surfaces suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Valverde De Leganés The most popular road cycling route is Llanos de Olivenza — circular from Valverde de Leganés, a 35.6 miles (57.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the open agricultural lands of the Llanos de Olivenza. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Táliga – Dehesas of the Olivenza River loop from Valverde de Leganés, a moderate 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This route offers views of the dehesas and the Olivenza River area. Local road cyclists also love the Valverde de Leganés loop from Valverde de Leganés, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Valverde de Leganés is defined by rural landscapes, olive groves, and dehesas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valverde de Leganés?
A: There are 9 road cycling routes available around Valverde de Leganés, offering a range of experiences from easy rides to more challenging tours.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Valverde de Leganés?
A: The region around Valverde de Leganés is characterized by a serene rural landscape, featuring extensive cultivated fields, olive groves, and dehesas. While many routes have mostly paved surfaces, some areas, particularly around the Piedra Aguda reservoir, can include varying elevations with challenging ascents and descents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy road cycling routes in the area. For a less strenuous option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38214271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivenza – Olivenza loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 25 km with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivenza – Castle of Olivença loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 138 km route with over 1800 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial workout.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivenza – Castle of Olivença loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 138 km route. Other moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995533604/llanos-de-olivenza-circular-from-valverde-de-leganes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llanos de Olivenza — circular from Valverde de Leganés&lt;/a&gt; (57 km) also provide a good distance.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Valverde de Leganés?
A: Cyclists can enjoy diverse scenery including extensive cultivated fields, olive groves, and dehesas. Notable natural features include the Piedra Aguda Reservoir and the impressive rock formations at "Los Canchales." You might also encounter historical paths like the Cañada Real Soriano Occidental. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Valverde de Leganés?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995533604/llanos-de-olivenza-circular-from-valverde-de-leganes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llanos de Olivenza — circular from Valverde de Leganés&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Táliga – Dehesas of the Olivenza River loop from Valverde de Leganés&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic rides through the local countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valverde de Leganés?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring the rural landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild but may have cooler, wetter days.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Valverde de Leganés?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the serene rural landscapes, the variety of terrain from flat fields to rolling hills, and the opportunity to explore traditional dehesas and olive groves.

Q: Are there any specific towns or settlements worth visiting along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; is a notable settlement that features in several routes. It offers historical charm and can be a great stop for a break or to explore its unique architecture.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Piedra Aguda Reservoir?
A: The Piedra Aguda Reservoir is a significant natural feature in the region. While some routes may pass nearby, the area around the reservoir is known for single-track trails, which might include unpaved segments. For a specific highlight, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1614952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Trail Along Embalse de Piedra Aguda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the dehesas (Mediterranean woodlands)?
A: Absolutely. The dehesas are a defining feature of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Táliga – Dehesas of the Olivenza River loop from Valverde de Leganés&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent route that specifically offers views of these unique Mediterranean woodlands and the Olivenza River area.
tips: Olivenza is one of the localities in Extremadura closest to the border with Portugal and where, since time immemorial, it has been fought over by both nations. The old town of white houses is beautiful, together with the castle of the same town and its church, the Parroquia de Santa María del Castillo.

It is in this area where the Río Olivenza crosses in the middle of forests of what is called "Dehesas extremeñas". Oak forests with little vegetation around and where cattle are found around, where the river gives life to these areas.
